programmes.show_description: (2021) Wes Anderson's star-encrusted tribute to the New Yorker magazine covers the final edition of a chaotic fictional publication, following the death of its editor - Bill Murray.
Town's Inn (Part 1/2): Gordon's at Harpers Ferry in West Virginia to visit The Town's Inn, a historic hotel that's so full of clutter it can barely accommodate guests.
It's the final. With a race across Britain and a £100k prize at stake, how do the pairs work together? Do they bond? Or do tensions boil over? And who will be Handcuffed champions? (Ep6)
Bart of Darkness: Bart suspects that Ned Flanders has killed his wife when he spies on him through the rear window.
Lisa's Rival: Lisa is jealous of the new smart girl at school (guest star Winona Ryder) and conspires with Bart to wreck her entry to the Dioramarama competition.
Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.
Kirstie and Phil visit west London, where property is expensive and sells fast. They help newly-weds Keon and Zoe in Shepherd's Bush, and Paul and Carolyn in Richmond.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Nicki Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Look Before You Sleep: While his apartment block is being fumigated, Sam can't go home, and he locks himself out of the bar. But he turns down Rebecca's offer to put him up.
Woody Gets an Election: Frasier has a bet against Sam that Woody would get at least 10% of the vote if he ran for city councillor. And then Woody's opponent faces a scandal...
It's Lonely on the Top: Carla's promoted to bartender and her drunken celebration leads to a night of passion with one of the Cheers regulars, but she can't recall who it was.
Robert's Wedding (Part 2/2): Robert and Amy exchange vows in a wedding ceremony that has the Barone family tradition written all over it.
Fun with Debra: Debra convinces Ray that she should join him on the golf course after she realises that they don't have fun together any more.
Thank You Notes: Marie tries to get the upper hand in her relationship with her new daughter-in-law when Amy and Robert return from their honeymoon.
Our Parents, Ourselves: Frasier plays Cupid when he arranges a supposedly accidental meeting between Martin and Roz's mother.
The Show Where Woody Shows Up: Frasier receives a surprise visit from Woody (guest star Woody Harrelson), his old friend from the Cheers bar in Boston.
Three Valentines: A crease in Niles's trousers leads to an escalating series of calamities as he prepares for a Valentine's date at Frasier's apartment.
To Tell the Truth: Niles hires Donny (Saul Rubinek), a legal shark and Roz's ex-boyfriend, to represent him in the divorce case.
Kings Blu Jam: With debts mounting, and a 'toxic' dynamic between owner Connie and her manager and daughter Justin, can Gordon turn things around at Kings Blu Jam Café in Texas?
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Herefordshire, Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo meet George, a collector for over 70 years. Max finds a crate of vintage motor oil bottles that could be worth a small fortune.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt reveals a big makeover project for the farm, but before they can start building, there's demolition to be done. And the farm celebrates a new addition.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Jon Kay in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Angie and Alan want to fulfil their dream of buying their very first property together on Spain's beautiful Orihuela Costa. Can Jasmine Harman find the perfect place for their £60k?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Spain, rookie hoteliers Jo and Ken hope to boost their business, and mum and daughter Becky and Kay start a top-to-bottom renovation of a B&B.
The new run continues. Shauna and Sam's Lancashire glampsite boasts a wealth of top-end facilities, including hot tubs, cinema screens and pizza ovens. Is it enough to see them victorious?
The new run continues in the West Country, where Kirstein hosts a Greek evening of hearty fare, plenty of spice, some tea towel tossing and terrific togas. (Ep1/5)

programmes.show_name: New: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: On Malta, Sara Damergi revisits Chris, who transformed a shell of an apartment into his dream place. And how are Jan and Bill getting on on the Costa Blanca?
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
...Cooking for Less: In Surrey, fitness-mad couple Nathan and Matt's contrasting cooking styles are draining their relationship. Can batching help them work out their differences?

programmes.show_name: New: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programmes.show_description: The epic adventure reality contest continues. The first player is culled, and another heart-pounding hunt begins. Will alliances count for anything in the thrill of the chase? (Ep2)
for Stand Up To Cancer: Joe Wilkinson, Roisin Conaty, Rose Matafeo, Judi Love, Tom Davis and Jon Richardson recreate the weirdest places that they've ever slept, in cake form!
Pansexual sex therapist Mariia's after a not-so-average Mr or Mrs who's not too tired for sex. Old romantic at heart Alannah seeks a forever fella who can speak her love language.
...Experiment: Tom and Kirsty check in to fulfil Tom's fantasy of experimenting with another man. Natalie and Tom explore a threesome, but will insecurities get in the way?
Beachfront Inn & Inlet: Gordon Ramsay visits a 'Caribbean-style' resort hotel in Fort Pierce, Florida, where guests face dusty rooms, late-night concerts and poor service.
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. Will Emma take Mac's offer to leave Fairley - and Edwin - for good? Adele's sobriety re-ignites her marriage. (Ep3/8)
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn and Jessica Reynolds. Emma starts a new life in Armley. And in the 1970s, she confronts her eldest daughter Edwina. (Ep4/8)
Danni Menzies helps Poppy and her dad Philip realise their dream of finding a holiday home in Andalucía for their budget of £50k, where all the family can enjoy the Spanish sunshine.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien's unique giant corkscrew wine rack seriously impresses valuer Ronnie.
Lucas faces a tough decision in order to protect himself from his cellmate. Dom holds the fort while Tony and Diane head to London for answers. And a revelation leaves Warren seething.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Jon Kay in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Rebecca Gaines, Rebecca Loses (Part 1/2): Rebecca impresses Kelly's father and is thrilled when he invites her over to his house, but the evening isn't what she was expecting.
Rebecca Gaines, Rebecca Loses (Part 2/2): Rebecca realises her obsession with rich men - like Mr Gaines - is doing her no favours. Cliff's mother goes into a retirement home.
The Guy Can't Help It: When Rebecca is attracted to a handsome plumber who comes to the bar, Sam's not very impressed. But Rebecca convinces Sam he's no great catch himself.
Home from School: When Michael stays home from school for two days in a row, Ray mistakenly suspects that he simply has homework he hasn't finished.
Misery Loves Company: Robert and Amy's over-the-top affection annoys Debra and Ray.
The Contractor: Debra persuades Ray to hire Gianni to do some DIY work in their kitchen, but they both come to regret it due to their friend's laziness.
Decoys: Niles discovers that Daphne is dating Donny, his divorce attorney, so he hatches a plan to get Donny back together with Roz.
Dinner Party: Frasier and Niles decide to throw a dinner party but can't agree on who they should invite.
Taps at the Montana: Niles wants his apartment back, but he must face up to both the snooty neighbours who think he's a menace and the person who's renting from him.
IQ: Who has the most impressive IQ - Frasier or Niles? The results of their childhood tests are finally revealed, and one is much higher than the other...
Verdict: In New Orleans, Gordon Ramsay visits family-run restaurant The Verdict, where the owner's 'evil' mother and chef frequently loses control of the kitchen - and her temper.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In the Devon countryside, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet collectors Derek and Daphne. Henry finds a very rare tractor, while Simon discovers a Raleigh Shopper bike.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Lambing season continues, as the family build a field shelter to keep an eye on the ewes. And Matt and Mike add some finishing touches to the stable yard.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Jon Kay in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Claire and Simon are starting a new adventure in Nerja in Spain. Will any of Craig Rowe's options for their £230k get their hearts racing enough to put in an offer?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Bulgaria, dad and son Colin and Paul turn a barn into an entertainment space. In Spain, Jo and Ken tackle a lull in business, and Becky and Kay prep for their first guests.
The new run continues. At luxury self-catering digs in Bradford, the B&Bers aren't sure that flat-pack furniture quite equates to the promised opulence.
The new run continues in the West Country. Peppe hosts an authentic Italian night, but one guest doesn't like Italian food! And is his hamster a serial killer? (Ep2/5)

programmes.show_name: New: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Is Uri and Hazel's vivacious villa on Cyprus still delivering the dream? And we see what Karmen has done to her meant-to-be Mijas bolthole.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Corina and Honey the cavapoo get off to a shaky start, but Honey has a trick or two that might just turn things around. And is Ruby the perfect teammate for football-mad Josh?

programmes.show_name: New: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programmes.show_description: The epic adventure reality contest continues. It's dog-eat-underdog as strategies shift and fortunes flip in the forest, and a shock twist puts even the strongest players in peril. (Ep3)
The Goggleboxers have been watching Married at First Sight Australia, Pangolin: Kulu's Journey, The Honesty Box, Silence Is Golden and Good Luck To You, Leo Grande.
This time the Goggleboxers watch Married at First Sight Australia, The Summit, LOL: Last One Laughing UK, Boarders, I Swear, Wheel of Fortune and Melania.
